methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MineSense Technologies is a Growth-stage frontier-tech company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private peers in the sector, including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Stage-progression methodology was the primary applicable approach. Using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x multiplier typical for Growth-stage progression to the next funding round, this method implies a valuation of $120M. The peer-median approach, while nine same-sector comps exist, could not be reliably applied: zero peers matched both stage and valuation tier filters simultaneously, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for meaningful triangulation. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and capital intensity within Climate & Energy.

The single-method triangulation yields a central estimate of $120M with a range of $102M to $138M. This range reflects modest sensitivity around the stage-progression step-up multiplier, absent richer peer anchoring. Key uncertainties include the lack of narrow-set comps at identical stage and valuation tier, which limits validation of the 1.2x multiplier assumption, and the dependence on total raised as a valuation proxy in the absence of disclosed interim round pricing. Validation through operational metrics or customer traction data would strengthen conviction in this range.
